item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -4 reminds me of factory porn

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the images are stills from The Night Porter, Fantastic Planet, and The Cremaster Cycle respectively, and are not under Public Domain. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: STAFF POST
item.content:encoded: Noting that revisions 17-21 were made without Authorial permission, and that they have been reverted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is more Keter than Euclid. The source of these films remains at large and unidentified, and a new one of these films could pop up anywhere at any time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s stuff like this that I look back on and discover that it’s much better then I thought it was back then.

-4’s dialogue is fucking chilling to the bone.

item.title: SCP-1127-2
item.content:encoded: SCP-1127-2's still is from the fantastic planet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could also be Mennonite or a "back to nature" commune.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"After exposure, subjects express an extreme discomfort with technological artifacts."

"After administration of class-A amnesiacs, any individuals to be released must be placed in an institutional environment or be relocated to [REDACTED] where they can be assimilated into the [REDACTED] population."

So..Amish population?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That first movie sounds like something created by Joker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well. I'm on some sort of watch list after googling all those fetish terms.

I gave +1 to this stuff.
It has the price to get +200, well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the second image from fantastic planet?

Also is it a bad sign when I kinda want to see all of the movies?

Also +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're probably right about the amnestics. Like I said; little subjective things. I've been trying really hard to make sense of the classifications. Possibly too hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having reread the article, I'm with you on the object class. As there are an unknown amount of movies out there with these effects, and we haven't found and/or contained them all, this should really be Euclid.

The amnestics though, you seem to have a very strong headcanon regarding them, but there is no site-wide concensus regarding classes, effects or even how they're administered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I really do; it's just the little subjective things that chew at me. Safe? Ignoring 4; which can turn people into cannibals, murderers, and paedophiles; 3 could affect thousands of people at once if properly administered and it isn't contained. (Picture it being played over a containment facility :o) Really just can't make peace with that classification. Also don't quite understand the use of class-A amnestics if it won't change the effect. Class-C, sure, but class-A?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The sample narrations perfectly captured the self-important, pseudo-intellectual tone of a young AWCY? anartist. Love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: people exposed to #4 that are released into society all the time. We call it the "Internet" dohhh ho ho ho ho ho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm new to the site… so when I see people bashing Are We Cool Yet? I think, "What? AWCY? is terrifying, just think about SCP-1127!"

This is deeply unsettling to me in a very personal way. For some reason, being forced to watch SCP-1127-1 or SCP-1127-4 are far more terrifying to me than watching SCP-1127-2 or SCP-1127-3. And all four are far scarier than being locked in a room with an angry, regenerating lizard or what-have-you. +1, and thanks for the nightmares.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You really nailed the AWCY vibe throughout the descriptions of the videos that I wasn't surprised to see the tag at the end, despite missing the hints in the title.

Thats very impressive +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfection. Simply that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
IMO that just means there are more uncontained "Safe" objects out there. In my headcannon, the classification is based on the resources required to contain something, not whether we actually caught up with it yet…

(After all, if MC&D stole one of their "Safe" artifacts back and sold it, would it stop being "Safe"?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is unknown how many more films in the SCP-1127 series have yet to be identified and recovered by the Foundation.

Then why isn't this Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was unsettling. Plenty of SCPs have that effect, but this does feel particularly violating without you having gone to any excessive lengths…it's just very penetrating.

Intriguing concept, particularly the bit about amnesiacs and the standards for releasing people into the general population (or not).

Egh. This leaves me feeling genuinely uncomfortable. +1, absolutely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel vaguely violated after reading this. Good work.

When our lives become the joke, humor becomes a war crime. The punch-line is always death, and to “get it” is to abandon the pretense that “getting it” matters. Laugh at the reality that is laughing at you!

For some reason I read this in Rod Serling's voice.
And then I became the 100th upvote. It's a thing that makes you crazy, but it's creative and unsettling and well-written. Bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any kind of film-based SCP is brilliant if written right.
This may be my favorite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: after going through and finding out what the those fetishes were, that last one became super creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was really creepy.

Though I had no idea that this was an AWCY? SCP until I saw the tag at the bottom. I guess I am too dense to get the titles reference(I got it after looking here and seeing people mentioning the titles)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I see it now. And it suddenly became much much scarier. XD One would only need broadcast it on public TV/internet in order to completely ruin a country.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's mentioned earlier that both fantasizing and pornography is no longer effective. (Though I suppose someone could pick up a pornography addiction.)

(And Wikipedia has a pretty exhaustive index of paraphilias.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was kind of surprised to see the size of the list of fetishes that was included. Did you Google "weird kinks"or something to make your list?

"Because of the necessity of the subject to act on these desires" confuses me a bit though. Could they use the internet for material or would they be forced to do the kink in real life in order to achieve sexual release?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see it wasn't only me that noticed that.

item.title: Re: Removed the addendum
item.content:encoded: I agree, especially after watching the debate in the ACWY? thread in the forums.

item.title: Re: Removed the addendum
item.content:encoded: I think its much better without the addendum. It just felt a bit forced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
various videos available on the internet including “█ █████ █ ███”.

Oh god.
Chocolate ice cream anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. Your writing style increased the creepiness factor, and I found myself afraid to click on each subsequent collapsible segment.

The SCP reminds me of Infinite Jest. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So youtube poop mixed with 4chan? Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I must be broken, because this really isn't doing anything for me. I read the whole thing and went, "Okay, that's nice. I like the cheetah woman." Even going back and noticing the AWCY reference (I knew it had to be related, but missed it the first time), I feel unmoved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is weird and disturbing and you should feel weird and disturbing. Or you can feel good. Either one works. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good stuff. And well, yeah, the addendum is unnecessary, especially since this has the are-we-cool-yet tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Best AWCY SCP since the anti-shark.

item.title: Re: Removed the addendum
item.content:encoded: I didn't notice the title dearly before you mentioned it. You have my downvote followed by an upvote so I can upvote this twice.

item.title: Re: Removed the addendum
item.content:encoded: I didn't notice the title dearly before you mentioned it. You have my downvote followed by an upvote so I can upvote this twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
::plucks a smoldering cigar from his mouth:: You see this?
::gesticulates wildly with hand holding the cigar:: YOU SEE THIS!?
::gets ashes in various area, leaves hazy trail of smoke:: THIS is how you make AWCY good!
::chomps furiously on cigar, goes back to what he was doing before::

(best envisioned as JK Simmons playing J Jonah Jameson, rather than my fat butt)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The connection remains. Look at the movie titles again.

item.title: Removed the addendum
item.content:encoded: Consensus was it detracted from the article. Like Raiche said, the movie titles were enough of a tie in. Please note, this is still AWCY? just more subtle than before…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be saddened by the loss of the addendum, actually. This makes an absolutely perfect example of how to do an AWCY article right, and while the loss of that link certainly won't weaken the content of the article, it'd be a shame to drop the connection between the two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's weird; this seems like something they would actually possibly do, but including them in this, or at least in such a "get it?" way like Raiche said just feels off.

Also, great article. +1 would vote again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SOmehow I knew there was going to be an "Are We Cool Yet?" in there somewhere. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Think we'll try it without the addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. This is great, and any time anyone can squeeze originality out of the "creepy video" genre I'm happy.

The only dissonant note was the AWCY reference at the very end; I generally don't care for the groups of interest, but that's more of a personal thing. But your last note suggests a definite source for the movies; FAR creepier when the reader doesn't know who is putting these out or why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was vacillating on that myself. If more people complain, I might get rid of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot, but not the Addendum. You have a link to AWCY in the movie titles already, the Addendum is like yelling "Get it?!" at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really don't like most of AWCY.

I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*starts slow clap……*
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man this is great. swann, you are pretty good at this scp thing, aren't you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
investigated by MTF Mu-53 (“Ebert's Thumb”)

I saw this and was already prepared to upvote.

… then I read the descriptions…

I don't feel safe anymore…

t-take the +1… I-I'll never doubt anyone is cool yet again…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I feel sullied and unusual.
